title: Purged race-group CV with 1-lap embargo
summary: The target is explicitly next-lap, so adjacent rows within the same driver-race-stint can leak label-near information even if race groups are separated imperfectly.
rationale: The target is explicitly next-lap, so adjacent rows within the same driver-race-stint can leak label-near information even if race groups are separated imperfectly. Purged/embargoed validation is widely used for event labels with overlapping horizons and should give a less optimistic CV estimate than ordinary grouped folds.
implementation_hint: Split by `Race_Year` or finer `Race_Year+Driver`, then purge training rows that are within 1 lap of any validation row for the same race-driver sequence; compare score drop vs current CV and trust the purged estimate for model selection.
expected_effect: May not raise raw CV, but should improve leaderboard alignment and reduce over-selection of brittle features.
risk: If the test set is less shifted than expected, stricter purging can underuse data and slightly lower fitted model quality.
